summary: Scorching Phil bass solo and tight jams, high‑energy Truckin' sequence
blurb: The show shines with a blistering Phil Lesh bass solo, a perfect Jerry Garcia tone in Playing In The Band, and a soaring second‑set jam that strings Truckin', Nobody's Fault But Mine, Eyes Of The World and Sugar Magnolia together. Soundboard recordings capture the performance in crystal‑clear detail, making it a must‑listen for fans of early‑’73 improvisation.
review: February 24, 1973 at the University of Iowa delivered one of the spring‑‘73 highlights. The first set rolled through classic Dead staples, but it was the second set that truly ignited the night. A scorching Phil Lesh bass solo – described by reviewers as “stereo magic” and a Wall‑of‑Sound marvel – launched an explosive jam that wove Truckin' into Nobody's Fault But Mine, then surged through Eyes Of The World before landing on a jubilant Sugar Magnolia. Jerry Garcia’s guitar work in Playing In The Band was praised as “perfect,” exploring uncharted territory with a tone that cut through the mix like a laser. Bill Kreutzmann’s cymbals were “right up in your face,” adding extra sparkle to the rhythm section, while Keith Godchaux and Donna Jean provide their steady backing. Even the rough audience tape captures a confident Wave That Flag, underscoring how consistently the band was locked in. With two excellent soundboard fragments that showcase pristine treble, crunchy bass, and flawless stereo separation, this show stands out as a vivid snapshot of the Dead at a creative peak.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are positive or mixed, with two clearly praising the performance (Phil's bass solo, Jerry's guitar tone) and the third noting strong jams despite audio roughness. No consistent negative comments appear, so a solid positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard fragment with crisp treble, crunchy bass and superb stereo separation that captures Phil's standout solo and the high‑energy jam sequence

key_highlights: Phil Lesh delivers a scorching, stereo‑rich bass solo that becomes the centerpiece of the night.
Jerry Garcia’s guitar tone in Playing In The Band is described as perfect and exploratory.
The second‑set mega‑jam strings Truckin', Nobody's Fault But Mine, Eyes Of The World and Sugar Magnolia into one seamless, high‑energy flow.
Wave That Flag stands out even on a rough audience tape, showing the band’s confidence throughout.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is praised as perfect, especially during the Playing In The Band improvisation where he explores uncharted territory.
band_performance.Phil: His bass solo is repeatedly called awesome and a highlight of the show, with stereo magic that showcases his powerful tone.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s cymbal work is highlighted as front‑and‑center, adding bright sparkle to the jam sections.
